title: Constitutional Emoluments Protection of American Interests Act of 2025
short_title: Constitutional Emoluments Protection of American Interests Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
plain_summary: This bill would stop the federal government from spending taxpayer money at any business or property owned or controlled by Donald Trump. The government would not be allowed to stay at Trump hotels, rent Trump buildings, or make any new deals with Trump companies. The goal is to prevent conflicts of interest between Trump's business interests and his role in government. This law would apply to all federal agencies and departments that handle taxpayer money.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
subjects: Government ethics and transparency, public corruption
Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Constitutional Emoluments Protection of American Interests Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Use of Funds
sections.text: The federal government cannot use taxpayer money at any property or business that is owned by, managed by, or controlled by Donald J. Trump. The government also cannot make new contracts, grants, or cooperative agreements with any of these properties or businesses. This includes a very long list of properties and companies connected to Donald Trump, such as Trump hotels, golf clubs, towers, and various business entities around the world. The list covers hundreds of properties and companies in places like New York, Florida, Chicago, Las Vegas, and many other locations both in the United States and in other countries.
